title: erodium absinthoides subsp. absinthoides
intro: Erodium absinthoides subsp. absinthoides is a subspecies of the Erodium absinthoides plant, commonly known as the "heron's bill" or "corn marigold," which belongs to the geranium family (Geraniaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and diuretic properties. The health benefits of Erodium absinthoides subsp. absinthoides include its ability to treat urinary tract infections, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of rheumatism. In herbal preparations, the plant can be made into a tea, infused in hot water, or used as a tincture to harness its medicinal properties.
